headline: What Exxon Mobil (XOM)'s Helium Edge and New Debt Deal Mean For Shareholders
summary: In late March 2026, Exxon Mobil completed a US$169.312 million offering of unsecured, unsubordinated floating rate notes due 2076, while also featuring its circular products business at the World Chemical Forum 2026 and reportedly exploring a breakup sale of its New Zealand operations. At the same time, Exxon Mobil has been benefiting from global oil and helium supply disruptions, with its LaBarge facility helping address the helium shortage. We’ll now examine how Exxon's strengthened...

headline: RBC expects these oil majors to gain from LNG price dislocations
summary: Investing.com -- Global LNG exports fell 4% quarter-on-quarter in the first three months of 2026 to 115 million tonnes, primarily due to Qatar’s March shutdown, though RBC Capital Markets said oil majors with large U.S. positions stand to capitalise on widening regional price spreads triggered by the conflict in Iran.
